title: PYDANTIC-0004 -- TzInfo(seconds) panics ('negate with overflow') on a large negative float via i32::MIN.abs(), instead of raising ValueError
crate: pydantic-core (own src); the overflow itself is i32::abs() in Rust core/num/mod.rs:426
expected: an out-of-range or non-finite seconds offset should raise ValueError (as -90000.0 already does); it must not panic
guarded_twin: the SAME range check returns the intended ValueError for every offset except i32::MIN: TzInfo(-90000.0) -> ValueError, and a large POSITIVE float saturates to i32::MAX (abs() is fine) -> ValueError too. Only the i32::MIN saturation makes seconds.abs() overflow. Fix = seconds.unsigned_abs() >= 86400 (u32, no overflow), or range-check without abs (e.g. !(-86400 < seconds && seconds < 86400)), or reject the saturating/non-finite cast up front.
reliability: deterministic; build-independent (ordinary i32::abs() overflow check, not a debug-only assert). py_new narrows to f32 first, so ANY float that narrows/casts to i32::MIN triggers it: -1e18, float('-inf'), -2147483648.0, even -2147483647.0 (rounds to i32::MIN in f32). float('nan') casts to 0 (OK); large POSITIVE floats saturate to i32::MAX and raise ValueError (no panic).
target_python: CPython 3.14t free-threaded + ASan; bug is interpreter/build independent
found_in: fusil-pydantic_core_03 (exploration mode: fusil_pydantic_plugin default, seeds off + known-crash suppression on). 255 crash dirs, ALL this one panic -- the first NOVEL finding after the known PYDANTIC-0001/0002/0003 were suppressed. The plugin's _pyd_fuzz_module_surface() calls TzInfo(-1e18).
prior_art: UNREPORTED (pydantic/pydantic-core tracker checked 2026-07-21 -- no issue for a TzInfo panic, negate/overflow, or this site). Maintainer stance per #1516: Python-reachable Rust panics are bugs to fix gracefully.
notes: TzInfo::py_new (src/input/datetime.rs:674) does `Self::try_from(seconds.trunc() as i32)` on `seconds: f32`. `float as i32` saturates in Rust, so a large negative float -> i32::MIN. TryFrom<i32>::try_from (datetime.rs:756) then does `if seconds.abs() >= 86400 { Err(ValueError...) }` -- and `i32::MIN.abs()` overflows (the panic is in Rust core/num/mod.rs:426, reached from datetime.rs:756). So the value IS out of range and SHOULD take the Err branch, but abs() panics first. Public API (pydantic_core.TzInfo). Panic-on-bad-input class.
